required_skills: AR and VR application development using Unity or Unreal Engine targeting headset platforms (Meta Quest, HoloLens, PlayStation VR, Apple Vision Pro)
XR interaction design and implementation including hand tracking, gaze input, spatial anchors, and 6DOF controller interaction patterns
Proficiency in C# (Unity) or C++ (Unreal) for XR application logic, interaction systems, and performance-critical code
VR rendering optimization techniques including foveated rendering, single-pass stereo rendering, and frame reprojection to maintain 72–120 fps headset targets
Spatial computing and world tracking using ARKit (iOS), ARCore (Android), or OpenXR for markerless AR and SLAM-based environment understanding
Performance profiling and optimization for mobile XR hardware (Meta Quest chipset, Apple M-series) under strict thermal and battery constraints
UI and UX design principles specific to immersive 3D environments including depth-appropriate placement, comfort zones, and locomotion sickness mitigation
OpenXR standard implementation for cross-platform headset compatibility and runtime abstraction
preferred_skills: Hand tracking and gesture recognition system development using Meta Hand Tracking SDK, Ultraleap, or Apple Vision Pro hand input APIs
Spatial audio implementation for immersive environments using binaural audio, HRTF processing, and ambisonics via FMOD or Wwise
Multiplayer VR experience development including avatar synchronization, shared spatial anchors, and networked interaction states
Computer vision and machine learning integration for AR scene understanding, object detection, and semantic segmentation on-device
Passthrough AR development on Meta Quest 3 or Apple Vision Pro for mixed reality experiences blending physical and digital environments
Digital twin and industrial XR application development for training, simulation, or remote assistance use cases
WebXR development for browser-based AR/VR experiences using Three.js, Babylon.js, or A-Frame frameworks
responsibilities: Design and develop immersive AR and VR applications for target headset platforms including Meta Quest 3, Apple Vision Pro, HoloLens 2, and PSVR2 using Unity or Unreal Engine
Implement XR interaction systems covering 6DOF controller input, hand tracking gestures, gaze-based selection, and spatial manipulation with physics-accurate object interaction
Optimize VR rendering pipelines to maintain stable frame rates at headset refresh targets (72–120 fps), implementing single-pass stereo, foveated rendering, and GPU-efficient draw strategies
Integrate spatial computing capabilities including SLAM-based world tracking, plane detection, spatial anchors, and occlusion handling using ARKit, ARCore, or OpenXR runtimes
Design and implement comfortable VR locomotion systems including smooth locomotion, teleportation, snap turning, and vignette comfort options that minimize vestibular dissonance
Build intuitive 3D spatial UIs with depth-appropriate menu placement, legible text at VR viewing distances, and interaction affordances that communicate clearly without a 2D screen paradigm
Profile XR application performance on mobile headset hardware using Oculus Performance tools and GPU profilers, resolving thermal throttling, CPU stalls, and overdraw bottlenecks
Develop multiplayer VR features including real-time avatar representation, shared object manipulation, and synchronized spatial anchor experiences using Photon or Unity Netcode
Collaborate with UX researchers to conduct VR usability testing sessions, gathering comfort, presence, and task-completion feedback and translating findings into interaction improvements
Evaluate and integrate emerging XR SDKs, platform APIs, and hardware capabilities from Meta, Apple, Microsoft, and Valve to expand the application's platform reach and feature set
